title: Iliamna longisepala
taxonomy: Iliamna longisepala belongs to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. This classification places it within the order Malvales and the clade Eurosids II. Iliamna longisepala is a species of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. It is a member of the genus Iliamna, which comprises about 20 species of plants. The specific epithet "longisepala" refers to the long stamens found in the plant.

intro: Iliamna longisepala, also known as Long-sepaled Iliamna, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of skin conditions and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Iliamna longisepala include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
